zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Rossell於大約1380年出生在英國英格蘭波克夏恩格利費爾德時，她的父親Walter Rossell22歲，母親Beatrice22歲。她於大約1404年與Philip Englefield結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1429年之後在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國波克夏恩格利費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Rossell于大约1380年出生在英国英格兰波克夏恩格利費爾德时，她的父亲Walter Rossell22岁，母亲Beatrice22岁。她于大约1404年与Philip Englefield结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1429年之后在她的家乡，埋葬在英国波克夏恩格利費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Rossell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rosell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Teobaldo, Jorge, Pedro, Santiago, Ana, Carlos, Ernesto, Jose, Miguel, Modesto, Alberto.  Scandinavian Erik, Nils.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element ros ‘rose’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .

variants.name: Rozzell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Rossell   or   Russell  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of   Rossel   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Rozzelle  .

variants.name: Rosselle
meanings: Altered form of English   Rossell  .
French: from the feminine form of   Rossel   and a variant of   Rousselle  . Compare   Rozelle  .

variants.name: Rosel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Felicitas, Araceli, Armando, Arturo, Cristina, Esequiel, Francisco, Margarita, Nestor, Paciano, Roberto, Rodelio.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .
German (Rösel): from a pet form of Rose 1.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Rossel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Juan, Alvaro, Ana, Carols, Catarino, Guillermo, Javier, Jose, Orlando, Pedro, Ruben.  French Philippe, Yves.  Dutch Gerrit, Flor.
French, Dutch, and Flemish: nickname for a red-haired man, from a diminutive of Old French and Dutch ros ‘red’.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Catalan   Rossell  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Rossell was born about 1380, in Englefield, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Walter Rossell, was 22 and her mother, Beatrice, was 22. She married Philip Englefield about 1404. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1429, in her hometown, and was buried in Englefield, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Rossell est née environ 1380, à Englefield, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter Rossell, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Beatrice, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Philip Englefield environ 1404. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1429, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Englefield, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Rossell um 1380 in Englefield, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter Rossell 22 und ihre Mutter Beatrice 22. Sie hat um 1404 Philip Englefield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1429 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Englefield, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Rossell è nata il circa 1380 nel Englefield, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Walter Rossell, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Beatrice, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Philip Englefield circa 1404. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1429, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolta a Englefield, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Rossellが約1380年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアエングレフィールドで生まれた時，父Walter Rossellは22歳，母Beatriceは22歳であった。約1404年にPhilip Englefieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1429年以後に故郷で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアエングレフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Rossell(이)가 대략 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 잉글레필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter Rossell(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1404년 Philip Englefield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1429년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 잉글레필드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Philip Englefield aproximadamente 1404. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1429, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em Englefield, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Rossell родилась около 1380 г. в местности энглефиелд, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Walter Rossell было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Englefield около 1404 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1429 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности энглефиелд, биркшир, Англия.
